title: Prepaid BIN Exploitation with Ghost Merchant Rotation
narrative: Fraudsters exploit prepaid card BINs (typically 403358, 448590, 486236 ranges) targeting MCCs with minimal verificationâ€”gas stations (5542), convenience stores (5411), and quick-service restaurants (5814). A single prepaid BIN produces 40-80 unique card numbers tested across 8-12 merchant IDs within 72 hours. Transactions cluster at $47-$89 to stay under automated review thresholds. The operation rotates through regional processors every 18-24 hours to avoid single-MID velocity triggers. Approval rates hit 68-74% because prepaid cards lack robust fraud controls and issuers can't easily contact cardholders. Email domains follow patterns like tempmail.org, guerrillamail.com, or missing entirely (null P_emaildomain in 83% of transactions). Chargebacks surface 14-21 days post-transaction when legitimate cardholders check balances. The attack concludes before issuers can block the BIN range system-wide.
indicators: Single IIN range (6-digit BIN) generates 40-80 unique card1 values within 72-hour window
TransactionAmt concentrated in $47-$89 band across 91% of transactions from affected BIN
card1_txn_count_merchant rotates: no single merchant exceeds 15 transactions per card
P_emaildomain null or disposable (tempmail, guerrilla, mailinator) in 78%+ of transactions
MCC distribution: 67% across 5542, 5411, 5814 with minimal high-risk category presence
Approval rate 68-74% vs. 52-58% baseline for same merchant cohort
dist1 values cluster in 3-5 geographic zones despite wide card1 distribution suggesting proxy use
summary: A single prepaid card BIN spawns 40-80 unique card numbers within 72 hours, all transacting $47-$89 at gas stations and convenience stores with 68-74% approval rates and mostly null or disposable email addresses.
reasoning: Fraudsters exploit weak prepaid card controls and rotate through multiple merchants to stay under velocity limits, conducting a fast-moving testing operation before issuers can block the compromised BIN range and before chargebacks reveal the fraud 14-21 days later.
indicator_explanations: Same bank identification number spawns dozens of different cards in days
Purchase amounts cluster tightly in the sweet spot below fraud thresholds
Each card spreads activity across merchants to avoid single-merchant flags
Email addresses are missing or belong to throwaway temporary mail services
Merchants are everyday categories like gas stations and fast food outlets
Fraudulent cards are getting approved far more than legitimate similar transactions
Locations appear diverse but actually funnel through handful of proxy addresses
